title: Bionano Genomics (BNGO) Q4 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Bionano Genomics reported Q4 2024 revenue of $8.2 million, down 24% year-over-year. GAAP gross margin improved to 42% from 23% in Q4 2023. Non-GAAP gross margin also improved to 42% from 24%. Operating expenses: $15.4 million GAAP; $10.6 million non-GAAP. Full Year 2024: Product revenues: $27 million (up 1% versus 2023), Consumables sales: $12.8 million (14% increase), Software sales: $6.2 million (11% increase), Flow cells sold: 30,307 units (15% increase year-over-year). Strategic Shift: The company transitioned away from aggressive customer acquisition toward supporting existing routine use customers. CEO Erik Holmlin explained: We're going to focus on these routine use customers, of which there are 118 exiting 2024. These customers generate approximately 80% of consumables revenue at roughly $85,000-$90,000 per customer annually. Key Business Pillars: 1. Support routine optical genome mapping users and VIA software adoption, 2. Drive utilization through VIA software expanding to over 160 OGM customer sites, 3. Build reimbursement support (Category 1 CPT code effective January 1, 2025), 4. Improve profitability through cost reduction and increased volume. 2025 Outlook: Expected revenue range: $29-$32 million, Q1 2025 revenue guidance: $6.2-$6.3 million, Planned installations: 15-20 new optical genome mapping systems, Cash runway extends into Q1 2026.
